subject: [PATCH v2 1/3] mm: kick writeback flusher instead of inline flush for IOCB_DONTCACHE

reviews.author: Ritesh (IBM)
reviews.summary: Reviewer Ritesh pointed out that the new API `filemap_dontcache_kick_writeback` gives a misleading sense of kicking writeback for only an inode's address space, when in fact it starts writeback for all dirty pages on the respective BDI. He suggested exporting the symbol for `wakeup_flusher_threads_bdi()` and using it instead, or modifying the API to make its purpose clearer.
reviews.raw_body:
This api gives a wrong sense that we are kicking writeback to write
dirty pages which belongs to only this inode's address space mapping.
But instead we are starting wb for everything on the respective bdi.

So instead why not just export symbol for wakeup_flusher_threads_bdi()
and use it instead?

If not, then IMO at least making it... 
   filemap_kick_writeback_all(mapping, enum wb_reason)

... might be better.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the current implementation of filemap_dontcache_kick_writeback causes a WB_SYNC_NONE writeback every time a DONTCACHE write finishes, which is not desired, and suggested introducing a new WB_start_dontcache bit to handle this case.
reviews.raw_body:
wakeup_flusher_threads_bdi ends up calling wb_start_writeback eventually,
which sets WB_start_all, pushes the reason to start_all_reason and then
does the actual wakeup.

The flusher thread then through wb_check_start_all does a WB_SYNC_NONE
writeback based on get_nr_dirty_pages.  Which seems wrong - we don't
want to do a huge writeback evertime the some DONTCACHE write finished.

So I think you'll want a new WB_start_dontcache bit, a new
get_nr_dontcache_pages() helper on a new node counter, etc.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Reviewer Christoph Hellwig suggested that the new WB_REASON_DONTCACHE reason for tracing visibility should not be exposed to userspace, and also requested that the function wakeup_flusher_threads_bdi() be marked as static in fs-writeback.c.
reviews.raw_body:
I'd rather not expose that to wide with the extra reason arguments
that's not otherwise exposed outside the core writeback code.

Btw, wakeup_flusher_threads_bdi should really be marked static in
fs-writeback.c as well.

2026-04-08.patch_summary: This patch addresses a performance issue in the IOCB_DONTCACHE writeback path by moving writeback submission off the writer's hot path. Instead of submitting writeback inline, it uses a background flusher thread to handle writeback asynchronously and coalescence I/O without explicit skip-if-busy or dirty pressure checks. The patch introduces a new function `filemap_dontcache_kick_writeback` that wakes up the BDI flusher thread with a specific reason for tracing visibility.
